PtIncident.Summary: Amendments to some late night Southeastern trains
PtIncident.Description:
Engineering work is taking place at various locations on the Southeastern network, closing some lines overnight from Monday to Thursday.

These changes may be subject to further alterations on some days, such as starting earlier, or not taking place at all. Please check your journey using the Journey Planner, and also check other items on our engineering pages for the date of your journey.

Mondays only:

Every Monday night, Network Rail carry out essential maintenance on the lines between Ashford International and Ramsgate via Canterbury West.

From 23:50 until the end of service, buses replace trains between Ashford International and Canterbury West / Ramsgate.

Mondays and Tuesdays:

Every Monday and Tuesday night, Network Rail carry out essential maintenance on the lines to and from London Charing Cross.

There will be no trains at London Charing Cross or London Waterloo East from approximately 23:30 until the end of service.

Trains will run from London Cannon Street instead, and you will need to travel to / from London Cannon Street / London Bridge.

Tuesdays only:

Every Tuesday night, Network Rail carry out essential maintenance on the lines between Faversham and Ramsgate via Margate.

From 23:30 until the end of service, buses replace trains between Faversham and Margate / Ramsgate.

Wednesdays and Thursdays:

Every Wednesday and Thursday night, Network Rail carry out essential maintenance on the lines to / from London Cannon Street.

There will be no trains at London Cannon Street from approximately 23:35 until the end of service.

Trains will run to / from London Charing Cross instead, if you could please make your way to London Bridge for these trains.

Ticket acceptance:

Monday to Thursday only:

From 23:00 until the end of service, you can use London Underground between Charing Cross, Embankment, Cannon Street, London Bridge, Southwark and Waterloo at no extra cost.

Check before you travel:

You can plan your journey using the National Rail Enquiries Journey Planner

You can plan your journey on London Underground by using the Transport for London Journey Planner

Replacement Bus Travel Advice:

For helpful advice if you need to travel on a rail replacement service, including accessibility and bicycle information, please use this page.

You can find the location of your bus replacement by checking station signs or by searching for your station on our station information pages.

Please be advised that, on occasion, replacement vehicles may be busier than usual, and you should allow extra time for your journey.

Affects.RoutesAffected: Various Southeastern routes

PtIncident.Summary: Buses replace trains between Corkickle and Whitehaven until further notice
PtIncident.Description:
During pre-planned engineering work at Bransty Tunnel, between Corkickle and Whitehaven, inspections revealed unstable ground conditions which require urgent repair. As a result, the line through the tunnel is closed.

Customer advice:

Service impact

Train services are running between Lancaster / Barrow-in-Furness and Corkickle, and also between Whitehaven and Carlisle.

Buses will replace trains between Corkickle and Whitehaven.

Please speak to station staff, see online journey planners or consult customer information screens for departure times of these buses. Journey times may be extended by up to 60 minutes whilst travelling by road transport.

Ticket acceptance

If the train you have a ticket for is cancelled, you can travel on any Northern or TransPennine Express service within two hours before or after your cancelled service was due to depart. This is at no extra cost, and does not require you to amend your ticket.

Additional information:

At the end of July 2025, Network Rail carried out assessments and surveys inside Bransty Tunnel ahead of planned full track and drainage renewal that was scheduled to take place during August and September. This involved doing surveys to assess any weight limits for the heavy machinery needed for the planned renewals. These surveys uncovered some areas under the railway which need strengthening before any work can progress. After assessing the initial survey data by specialist expert engineers, Network Rail has closed Bransty Tunnel to all trains until further notice. The exact time frame for how long these repairs and subsequent track improvements will take is still being worked on.

Check before you travel:

You can check your journey using the National Rail Enquiries real-time Journey Planner.

For helpful advice if you need to travel on a rail replacement service, including accessibility and bicycle information, please use our Rail Replacement Services page.

You can find the pick-up and set-down location of rail replacement services by checking station signage or by searching for your station on our Find a Station page.

Please be advised that replacement vehicles may be busier than usual, and you should allow extra time for your journey.

Affects.RoutesAffected: Between Lancaster / Barrow-in-Furness and Carlisle

PtIncident.Summary: Transpennine Route Upgrade: Buses replace trains between Huddersfield / Halifax and Leeds via Dewsbury from Saturday 30 May to Friday 26 June
PtIncident.Description:
Engineering work is taking place between Huddersfield / Halifax and Leeds, closing all lines via Dewsbury / Brighouse. Background information on this work can be found below.

Northern:

Saturday and Sunday:

Buses will replace trains between:

  • Halifax and Leeds via Brighouse / Dewsbury
  • Huddersfield and Leeds via Dewsbury

An amended train service will run between Manchester Victoria and Leeds, running via Bradford Interchange. This service will not call at stations via Dewsbury / Brighouse.

Monday to Friday:

Buses will replace trains between:

  • Huddersfield and Leeds
  • Huddersfield and Brighouse

TransPennine Express:

Saturday and Sunday:

The following train plan will operate between:

  • Leeds and Newcastle
  • Leeds and Redcar Central
  • Wakefield Kirkgate and York
  • Leeds and Hull
  • York and Scarborough
  • Manchester Airport and Huddersfield
  • Liverpool Lime Street and Huddersfield
  • Manchester Victoria and Huddersfield (calling at Todmorden, Hebden Bridge, Mytholmroyd, Sowerby Bridge)
  • Leeds and York (calling at Cross Gates, Garforth, East Garforth and Micklefield)

Replacement buses will run between:

  • Huddersfield and Wakefield Kirkgate
  • Huddersfield and Leeds via Dewsbury

Monday to Friday:

The following train plan will operate between:

  • Liverpool Lime Street and Newcastle (does not stop at Newton-le-Willows, but will additionally call at Lea Green)
  • Liverpool Lime Street and Manchester Victoria (calling at Newton-le-Willows)
  • Manchester Airport and Scarborough
  • Leeds and Hull
  • Manchester Piccadilly and York
  • Manchester Piccadilly and Huddersfield
  • York and Redcar Central

Replacement buses will run between:

  • Huddersfield and Leeds via Dewsbury

Background information:

Transpennine Route Upgrade work is taking place between Bradley Junction (which is near Deighton and Mirfield) and Leeds, where teams are carrying out track and drainage renewals to improve reliability. Overhead line equipment works are also ongoing to support future electrification.

At Batley station, teams are extending platforms to 150 metres, alongside resurfacing and coping stone realignment. Work is also progressing on the subway infill and lift installation to improve accessibility.

At Dewsbury station, platform extensions to 200 metres are underway, along with resurfacing and coping stone realignment. Canopy extensions, subway strengthening and footbridge improvements are also progressing.

Elsewhere, reconstruction of the Jack Lane railway bridge in Batley is ongoing as part of the wider upgrade works.

More information on this project is available from the Transpennine Route Upgrade website.

Check before you travel:

You can plan your journey using the National Rail Enquiries Journey Planner

You can find the pick-up and set-down location of rail replacement services by checking station signage or by searching for your station on our Find a Station page.

Please be advised that replacement vehicles may be busier than usual, and you should allow extra time for your journey.

Affects.RoutesAffected:
Northern between Wigan Wallgate / Wigan North Western / Huddersfield / Bradford Interchange / Halifax / Brighouse and Leeds

All TransPennine Express services to / from / via Huddersfield

PtIncident.Summary: Amended late night / early morning Southeastern services between Monday 8 and Friday 12 June
PtIncident.Description:
Engineering work is taking place overnight at various locations on the Southeastern network, closing some lines.

Monday, Wednesday and Thursday:

The 23:40 London Cannon Street to Dover Priory (Monday) / 23:34 London Charing Cross to Canterbury West / Dover Priory (Wednesday / Thursday) will be diverted between London Bridge and Ashford International, and will run non-stop between London Bridge and Ashford International.

Accessible replacement buses will run between:

  • Tonbridge and Headcorn (calling at all stations)
  • Headcorn and Dover Priory calling at Pluckley, Folkestone West and Folkestone Central
  • Ashford International and Canterbury West calling at all stations (will connect with the bus from Headcorn at Ashford International)

If you are travelling to Sevenoaks, Tonbridge, or connecting to the replacement bus at Tonbridge, please use alternative trains between London, Sevenoaks and Tonbridge.

Monday to Thursday:

From approximately 23:20, some trains will arrive / depart London Victoria later than usual, by up to 8 minutes with longer journeys on some trains to Ashford International, Dartford and Orpington.

From 23:30 until the end of service, accessible buses will replace trains between Bromley South / Swanley and Rochester.

The 23:40 London Victoria to Ramsgate train will run non stop between London Victoria and Rochester. An additional train will run from London Victoria to Bromley South (departing at 23:43) to connect with replacement buses between Bromley South and Meopham.

The 00:10 and 00:40 London Victoria to Gillingham (Kent) trains will run non-stop between London Victoria and Rochester. If you are travelling from either Bromley South, Swanley, Longfield and Meopham to stations beyond Gillingham (Kent), you will need to travel earlier than usual.

An additional train will run between London Victoria and Swanley, leaving at 00:40 to connect with the replacement bus services between Swanley, Meopham and Gillingham.

Accessible buses will run between:

  • Swanley and Meopham, calling at all stations
  • Meopham and Gillingham (Kent), calling at Rochester and Chatham
  • Meopham and Sole Street*

*If you are travelling to or from Sole Street and have additional assistance requirements we recommend you contact the Southeastern accessible travel team, send #Assist to 07866 002960 on WhatsApp or use the help point at the station so Southeastern can ensure appropriate transport is available for you.

Tuesday and Wednesday:

The 00:26 London Cannon Street to Dartford via Greenwich will be diverted to run via Bexleyheath. An accessible bus will run between London Bridge and Dartford via Greenwich You will need to use an alternative train from London Cannon Street to London Bridge to connect with the bus.

Wednesday and Thursday:

The 22:34 London Charing Cross to Ramsgate via Canterbury West train will be replaced by an accessible bus between Minster and Ramsgate calling at Thanet Parkway.

The 23:07 London St Pancras International to Ramsgate via Dover Priory service will be replaced by an accessible bus between Dover Priory and Ramsgate calling at all stations.

The 23:40 London St Pancras International to Margate via Canterbury West service will be replaced by an accessible bus between Canterbury West and Margate calling at all stations except Dumpton Park.

Thursday and Friday:

The 00:22 London Charing Cross to Dartford via Woolwich Arsenal will be diverted to run via Bexleyheath. An accessible bus will run between Lewisham and Dartford via Woolwich Arsenal. You will need to use an alternative train from London Charing Cross, Waterloo East or London Bridge to Lewisham to connect with the bus.

Check before you travel:

You can plan your journey using the National Rail Enquiries Journey Planner

PtIncident.Summary: No late night / early morning services via Seven Sisters from Monday 8 to Friday 12 June
PtIncident.Description:
Engineering work is taking place between Hackney Downs and Cheshunt, closing all lines via Seven Sisters overnight from Monday evening until Friday morning.

Greater Anglia / Stansted Express:

Between 23:35 and 05:15 each night, trains which usually run via Seven Sisters will be diverted to run via Tottenham Hale instead.

Stations on the Tottenham Hale line are only a short distance from those on the Seven Sisters line. You can view maps that show routes between these stations on the Greater Anglia website.

London Overground:

After 23:35 until the end of service on Monday to Thursday evenings, no trains will run between London Liverpool Street and Enfield Town / Cheshunt.

The last trains to run will be:

  • 22:43 London Liverpool Street to Cheshunt service (Monday, Tuesday and Thursday)
  • 22:13 London Liverpool Street to Cheshunt service (Wednesday)
  • 22:50 Enfield Town to London Liverpool Street
  • 22:58 London Liverpool Street to Enfield Town
  • 22:59 Cheshunt to London Liverpool Street

If you have a National Rail paper ticket or e-ticket, you can use your ticket at no extra cost on the following operators’ services:

  • Central line for Bethnal Green
  • Local bus 254 between Bethnal Green Central Line station and Cambridge Heath, London Fields and Hackney Downs
  • Mildmay line for Hackney Downs (Hackney Central)
  • Local bus 276 for journeys between Hackney Downs and Rectory Road
  • Local buses 106 or 393 for journeys between Clapton and Stoke Newington
  • Local buses 243 and 254 for journeys between Seven Sisters and Stamford Hill
  • Victoria line to / from Seven Sisters
  • Local buses 149, 249, 279 and 349 for journeys between Seven Sisters, Bruce Grove, White Hart Lane, Silver Street and Edmonton Green
  • Local bus 192 for journeys between Edmonton Green, Bush Hill Park and Enfield Town
  • Local bus 349 for journeys between Edmonton Green and Southbury
  • Local bus 279 for journeys between Edmonton Green and Turkey Street
  • Local buses 279 and 251 for journeys between Turkey Street and Theobalds Grove
  • Local buses 251 / 310 for journeys between Theobalds Grove and Cheshunt
  • Mildmay line to Hackney Central and local bus 253 for Clapton
  • Victoria line to Walthamstow Central and local buses:
    • W11 / 275 for St James Street
    • 230 or W15 and W16 to Wood Street
    • Local bus 212 between Chingford and Highams Park
    • Local buses 97 / 212 between Chingford and Walthamstow Central

If you are using contactless payment / Oyster card, you can also use the above alternative travel options but you will be required to pay the normal tap in / tap out fares for your journey.

Check before you travel:

You can plan your journey using the National Rail Enquiries Journey Planner

You can plan your journey on London Buses / London Underground by using the Transport for London Journey Planner

Affects.RoutesAffected:
Greater Anglia between London Liverpool Street and Cheshunt / Enfield Town

London Overground between London Liverpool Street and Enfield Town / Cheshunt

PtIncident.Description:
Engineering work is taking place overnight between Bedford and Luton and between Leicester and East Midlands Parkway, closing some lines.

Monday to Friday:

The 22:47 London St Pancras International to Corby train will depart Bedford later than scheduled at 23:47, it will continue to run up to 12 minutes later through to Corby.

Monday to Thursday:

The 21:35 London St Pancras International to Nottingham train will depart East Midlands Parkway later than scheduled at 23:06, it will continue to run up to 14 minutes later through to Nottingham.

The 22:32 London St Pancras International to Sheffield train will depart Loughborough later than scheduled at 23:57, it will continue to run up to 13 minutes later through to Sheffield.

The 23:04 London St Pancras International to Derby train will depart Leicester earlier than scheduled at 01:33, it will then depart Loughborough later than scheduled at 01:53. It will continue to run up to 12 minutes later through to Derby.

The 23:35 London St Pancras International to Nottingham train will depart Kettering later than scheduled at 01:04, it will continue to run up to 21 minutes later through to Nottingham.

Tuesday to Friday:

The 04:14 Derby to London St Pancras International train will depart earlier from Derby at 04:00, it will continue to run up to 14 minutes earlier through to Luton Airport Parkway.

Friday:

The 21:00 Sheffield to London St Pancras International train will arrive at London St Pancras International earlier than scheduled at 23:08.

The 23:35 London St Pancras International to Nottingham train will depart Kettering earlier than scheduled at 00:55, it will then depart Leicester later then scheduled at 01:52, it will continue to run up to 24 minutes later through to Nottingham. It will not call at Market Harborough. Rail replacement buses will run between Kettering and Market Harborough and Market Harborough and Leicester for connections.

PtIncident.Summary: Berrylands Station Upgrade: Buses replace trains to / from Berrylands from Monday 11 May to the end of September
PtIncident.Description:
Engineering work is taking place at Berrylands station, closing the station from Monday 11 May to the end of September. Background information on the work can be found below.

As a result, no trains will call at Berrylands station. Trains will still be able to run through the station without stopping.

Replacement buses will run between:

  • New Malden and Berrylands
  • Berrylands and Surbiton

Please be aware that on some weekends, engineering work will close all lines through Berrylands. You can check for engineering items via the Status and Disruptions page.

Background information:

Network Rail are carrying out essential upgrades at Berrylands station, to extend the life of the station and improve the experience for passengers travelling via this important suburban hub. To allow completion of the work, Berrylands station will be closed for over 4 months and trains will run through without stopping.

Berrylands station sits on a five-metre-high embankment with timber platforms that are no longer fit for purpose. This project will see the full replacement of Berrylands’ two timber platforms and supporting structures, which date back to the 1930s. Also, narrow sections of the platform will be widened to improve safety and accessibility. New platform lighting, CCTV, information screens and help points will be installed. This project will ensure Berrylands continues to serve the local community reliably and safely for years to come. More details on the work taking place are available on the Network Rail website.

You can plan your journey using the National Rail Enquiries Journey Planner

PtIncident.Summary: Far North Line upgrades: buses will replace trains between Dingwall and Wick from late on Sunday 7 to early on Monday 29 June
PtIncident.Description:
Engineering work is taking place between Dingwall and Wick, closing all lines. Background information on the work can be found below.

Monday 8 to Sunday 28 June

Inverness – Wick / Thurso:

Full size coaches will operate between Inverness and Wick.

Minibuses connect with coaches at Tain, Golspie and Helmsdale.

A limited amount of train services will operate from Inverness to Dingwall only, calling at intermediate stations.

You can also view a map of this work via the link.

Inverness – Kyle of Lochalsh:

Trains will operate as normal.

Please note: the following trains on Sunday 7 and Monday 29 June are also cancelled:

Sunday 7 June:

  • 17:54 Inverness to Wick

Monday 29 June

  • 06:18 Wick to Inverness
  • 08:02 Wick to Inverness

Background information:

Network Rail is set to carry out major engineering work worth £6m on the Far North Line, to help improve journeys on one of the Highlands’ most important transport routes. Upgrades will take place at several locations including between Evanton (near Alness), Tain, and Lairg, and on sections north of Tain towards Wick and Thurso.

Engineers will be renewing more than 4 km of track, carrying out repairs to a railway bridge, improving drainage, and maintaining the level crossing at Rogart.

In order for the work to be completed safely and efficiently, no trains will run between Dingwall and Wick from late on Sunday, 7 June, until early on Monday, 29 June. During this time, ScotRail will operate a rail replacement plan. More information on the work can be found on the Network Rail website.

PtIncident.Summary: Building new footbridge with lifts: Amended services to / from Tackley from Friday 12 June 2026 until Friday 30 April 2027
PtIncident.Description:
Engineering work is taking place on the footbridge at Tackley, closing platform 2.

From Friday 12 June 2026 through until Friday 30 April 2027, trains in the Oxford direction (southbound) will not stop at Tackley station. This is so that a permanent footbridge with lifts can be installed at the station for passengers to cross between the platforms (see background information below).

Trains running towards Banbury (northbound) will continue to call at Tackley as normal.

If you are travelling from Tackley towards Oxford, your travel options are as follows:

  • Travel from Heyford station instead (3.5 miles away by road)
  • Catch a train northbound and change at Heyford station. You should cross to the opposite platform upon arrival at Heyford station, and catch a southbound train towards Oxford
  • Catch the Stagecoach S4 bus service from Tackley village which stops at Oxford Parkway station for onward train connections before continuing to Oxford city centre

If you are travelling to Tackley from the Banbury direction, your travel options are as follows:

  • Travel from Heyford station instead (3.5 miles away by road)
  • Catch the Stagecoach S4 bus service from Banbury bus station (approximately 6 minute distance from Banbury station) to Tackley village
  • Catch a train to Heyford station then travel a short distance to Heyford Bridge bus stop and catch the Stagecoach S4 bus service to Tackley village from there

Please note, if you choose to travel using the Stagecoach bus route mentioned above, this will be at your own cost.

Great Western Railway has implemented a discounted £2.10 ticket from Oxford to Tackley to ensure customers do not pay anymore during the platform closure. This will ensure that if a customer used the S4 bus to get into Oxford (£3) and the train to get home (£2.10) they would only pay £5.10, the current price of an off-peak return rail ticket.

Please note: You will need to claim the discount at the ticket office at Oxford station.

If you are a disabled passenger or a passenger with reduced mobility or additional needs and you are wishing to travel to or from Tackley, please call Great Western Railway's passenger assistance team on 0800 197 1329.

Background information:

In June 2026, work will begin to build a new footbridge with two lifts at Tackley station. The bridge will be built on the site of the current, temporary, scaffold bridge. Network Rail aim to open the new bridge in spring 2027, with the lifts to follow later in the year.

To allow the new bridge to be built the southbound platform, for trains towards Oxford, will be closed. No trains will call at this platform until the new bridge opens. More information on the work taking place, and how it affects Tackley village, can be found on the Network Rail website.

Check before you travel:

You can plan your journey using the National Rail Enquiries Journey Planner.

You can plan your journey on buses by using the Traveline website.

Affects.RoutesAffected: All Great Western Railway services to / from Tackley

PtIncident.Summary: Buses replace trains between Leamington Spa / Rugby and Nuneaton / Birmingham International on Sunday 14 June
PtIncident.Description:
Engineering work is taking place between Rugby and Birmingham International, closing all lines.

Avanti West Coast:

Trains between London Euston and Birmingham New Street / Wolverhampton will not run.

Watford Junction and Rugby stations will be served by trains running between London Euston and Liverpool Lime Street / Manchester Piccadilly instead. Trains between London Euston to Crewe / Preston / Scotland via Birmingham New Street will start / terminate at Birmingham International.

Rail replacement buses will operate between Rugby and Birmingham International, calling at Coventry.

CrossCountry:

Trains will be diverted, and will not call at Coventry or Birmingham International.

Replacement buses will run between Leamington Spa and Birmingham International via Coventry.

London Northwestern Railway:

Train services between London Euston / Northampton and Birmingham New Street will run between London Euston / Northampton and Rugby and also between Birmingham International and Birmingham New Street only.

Trains between Coventry and Birmingham New Street will run between Birmingham International and Birmingham New Street only.

Rail replacement buses will operate between Rugby / Coventry and Birmingham International.

West Midlands Railway:

Rail replacement buses will operate between Leamington Spa and Nuneaton.

Check before you travel:

You can plan your journey using the National Rail Enquiries Journey Planner

PtIncident.Summary: Amended late night Northern service to / from Manchester Piccadilly from Monday 15 to Thursday 18 June
PtIncident.Description:
Engineering work is taking place overnight between Hadfield and Manchester Piccadilly, closing some lines.

Monday to Thursday:

The 22:17 Stoke-on-Trent to Manchester Piccadilly service will terminate at Stockport. Onward train services are available.

The 22:45 Sheffield to Manchester Piccadilly service will not call at New Mills Central, Strines, Marple, Romiley, Bredbury, Brinnington, Reddish North, Ryder Brow, Belle Vue and Ashburys, and will call additionally at Stockport. A bus will be provided from Chinley to Manchester Piccadilly to serve intermediate stations.

The 23:16 Manchester Piccadilly to Macclesfield service will not run. The 21:55 Blackpool North to Manchester Airport service will be extended to Macclesfield in its place.

The 23:18 New Mills Central to Manchester Piccadilly train will not run and will be replaced by a bus.

The 23:25 Manchester Piccadilly to New Mills Central train will not run and will be replaced by a bus.

The 23:29 Manchester Piccadilly to Glossop service will not run. A bus will be provided between Manchester Piccadilly and Hadfield, calling at Ashburys, Gorton, Guide Bridge, Flowery Field, Newton for Hyde, Godley, Glossop and Dinting. A mini-bus will be provided between Godley and Hadfield, calling at Hattersley, Broadbottom, Glossop and Dinting.

The 23:37 Manchester Piccadilly to Manchester Airport service will not run. The 22:22 Blackpool North to Manchester Airport service will call additionally at Mauldeth Road, Burnage, East Didsbury, Gatley and Heald Green.

Check before you travel:

You can plan your journey using the National Rail Enquiries Journey Planner

PtIncident.Description:
Engineering work is taking place between Leeds and Micklefield / Selby, closing all lines.

CrossCountry:

Except for trains which start / terminate at Leeds, trains will not call at Leeds or Wakefield Westgate. Trains will be diverted to run via Doncaster, additionally calling there.

You can use alternative train services at no extra cost for travel to / from Leeds / Wakefield Westgate.

Northern:

Trains between Blackpool North and York will terminate / start from Leeds. A replacement bus service will operate between Leeds and York.

Trains between Halifax and Hull will run between Halifax and Leeds and between Selby and Hull. A replacement bus service will operate between Leeds and Selby.

TransPennine Express:

An amended train service will run between Leeds and Hull via Castleford, and between Leeds and Redcar Central via Castleford. These diverted trains will have extended journey times.
